questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: In the shown figure $m_A, m_B$ and $m_C$ are the masses of the three blocks, the incline is frictionless $m_A=5 \mathrm{~kg}, m_B=10 \mathrm{~kg}, m_C=2 \mathrm{~kg}$. coefficient of friction between $A$ and $B$ is 0.5 and between $B$ and $C$ is $0.1\left(\right.$ Take $\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ )
On the basis of above data answer the following questions.Question: What is the frictional force on block A
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: A block of mass 10 kg is put gently on a belt-conveyor system of infinite length at $\mathrm{t}=0 \mathrm{sec}$, which is moving with constant speed 20 $\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{sec}$ rightward at all time, irrespectively of any situation by means of a motor-system as shown in the figure. Constant force of magnitude 15 N is applied on the block continuously during its motion.
Question: The nature of friction force acting on the block of mass 10 kg during its motion is
options: some time kinetic some time static
questions.solution:
In the time interval $t=0 \mathrm{sec}$ to $t=t_0 \mathrm{sec}$, till the time. The relative velocity is not zero the nature of friction will be kinetic. $\begin{aligned} & \overrightarrow{\mathrm{u}}_{A B}=0 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-(20 \hat{\mathrm{i}})=20 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{sec}(\hat{\mathrm{i}}) \\ & \mathrm{N}=100 \mathrm{~N} \Rightarrow \mathrm{F}_{\mathrm{k}}=0.25 \times 100=25 \mathrm{~N}\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \vec{a}_A=4 \mathrm{~m} / \sec ^2(\hat{\mathrm{i}}), \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_{\mathrm{B}}=0 \mathrm{~m} / \sec ^2(\hat{\mathrm{i}}) \\ & \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_{\mathrm{AB}}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_{\mathrm{A}}-\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_{\mathrm{B}}=4 \mathrm{~m} / \sec ^2(\hat{\mathrm{i}}) \\ & \overrightarrow{\mathrm{V}}_{\mathrm{AB}}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{u}}_{\mathrm{AB}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_{\mathrm{AB}} \mathrm{t}=\overrightarrow{0} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{t}=5 \mathrm{sec}\end{aligned}$
When the relative motion between block $A$ and belt conveyor will be zero, the nature of friction will be static and its magnitude will equal to magnitude of unbalanced external force acting on the block A and its direction will be I the opposite direction of unbalanced external force. $\Rightarrow F_r=$ Frictional force $=\left\{\begin{array}{l}25 N(\hat{i}) \Rightarrow \text { kinetic in nature } \\ 15 N(-\hat{i}) \Rightarrow \text { Static in nature }\end{array}\right.$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q:
Paragraph: A block of mass 10 kg is put gently on a belt-conveyor system of infinite length at $\mathrm{t}=0 \mathrm{sec}$, which is moving with constant speed 20 $\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{sec}$ rightward at all time, irrespectively of any situation by means of a motor-system as shown in the figure. Constant force of magnitude 15 N is applied on the block continuously during its motion.
Question: Work done by the kinetic friction on the block of mass 10 kg is
questions.solution:
In the time interval $t=0 \mathrm{sec}$ to $t=t_0 \mathrm{sec}$, till the time. The relative velocity is not zero the nature of friction will be kinetic. $\begin{aligned} & \overrightarrow{\mathrm{u}}_{A B}=0 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-(20 \hat{\mathrm{i}})=20 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{sec}(\hat{\mathrm{i}}) \\ & \mathrm{N}=100 \mathrm{~N} \Rightarrow \mathrm{F}_{\mathrm{k}}=0.25 \times 100=25 \mathrm{~N}\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \vec{a}_A=4 \mathrm{~m} / \sec ^2(\hat{\mathrm{i}}), \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_{\mathrm{B}}=0 \mathrm{~m} / \sec ^2(\hat{\mathrm{i}}) \\ & \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_{\mathrm{AB}}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_{\mathrm{A}}-\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_{\mathrm{B}}=4 \mathrm{~m} / \sec ^2(\hat{\mathrm{i}}) \\ & \overrightarrow{\mathrm{V}}_{\mathrm{AB}}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{u}}_{\mathrm{AB}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_{\mathrm{AB}} \mathrm{t}=\overrightarrow{0} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{t}=5 \mathrm{sec}\end{aligned}$
When the relative motion between block $A$ and belt conveyor will be zero, the nature of friction will be static and its magnitude will equal to magnitude of unbalanced external force acting on the block A and its direction will be I the opposite direction of unbalanced external force. $\Rightarrow F_r=$ Frictional force $=\left\{\begin{array}{l}25 N(\hat{i}) \Rightarrow \text { kinetic in nature } \\ 15 N(-\hat{i}) \Rightarrow \text { Static in nature }\end{array}\right.$

questions.q:
Paragraph: A block of mass 10 kg is put gently on a belt-conveyor system of infinite length at $\mathrm{t}=0 \mathrm{sec}$, which is moving with constant speed 20 $\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{sec}$ rightward at all time, irrespectively of any situation by means of a motor-system as shown in the figure. Constant force of magnitude 15 N is applied on the block continuously during its motion.
Question: The magnitude of acceleration of the block of mass 10 kg at $\mathrm{t}=6 \mathrm{sec}$ is
questions.solution:
In the time interval $t=0 \mathrm{sec}$ to $t=t_0 \mathrm{sec}$, till the time. The relative velocity is not zero the nature of friction will be kinetic. $\begin{aligned} & \overrightarrow{\mathrm{u}}_{A B}=0 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-(20 \hat{\mathrm{i}})=20 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{sec}(\hat{\mathrm{i}}) \\ & \mathrm{N}=100 \mathrm{~N} \Rightarrow \mathrm{F}_{\mathrm{k}}=0.25 \times 100=25 \mathrm{~N}\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \vec{a}_A=4 \mathrm{~m} / \sec ^2(\hat{\mathrm{i}}), \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_{\mathrm{B}}=0 \mathrm{~m} / \sec ^2(\hat{\mathrm{i}}) \\ & \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_{\mathrm{AB}}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_{\mathrm{A}}-\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_{\mathrm{B}}=4 \mathrm{~m} / \sec ^2(\hat{\mathrm{i}}) \\ & \overrightarrow{\mathrm{V}}_{\mathrm{AB}}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{u}}_{\mathrm{AB}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_{\mathrm{AB}} \mathrm{t}=\overrightarrow{0} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{t}=5 \mathrm{sec}\end{aligned}$
When the relative motion between block $A$ and belt conveyor will be zero, the nature of friction will be static and its magnitude will equal to magnitude of unbalanced external force acting on the block A and its direction will be I the opposite direction of unbalanced external force. $\Rightarrow F_r=$ Frictional force $=\left\{\begin{array}{l}25 N(\hat{i}) \Rightarrow \text { kinetic in nature } \\ 15 N(-\hat{i}) \Rightarrow \text { Static in nature }\end{array}\right.$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A bike of length $\ell$ having its centre of mass at the middle is to turn with constant speed v [here v is the speed of centre of mass of the bike]. To turn, the front wheel is rotated by an angle $\theta$. The coefficient of friction between the tyres and the road is $\mu$. Assuming the bike to be vertical and it does not skid, answer the following questions.The radius of curvature of the centre of mass of the bike is
questions.solution: If the bike does not slip, path of both the wheel must be parallel with its direction as shown in the figure. $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{R}_1=\ell / \tan \theta \\ & \mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{cm}}=\sqrt{\left(\frac{\ell}{2}\right)^2+\mathrm{R}_1^2}=\frac{\ell}{2 \tan \theta} \sqrt{4+\tan ^2 \theta} \\ & \text { Net friction force } \mathrm{f}=\frac{\mathrm{mv}^2}{\mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{cm}}}=\frac{2 \mathrm{~m} v^2 \tan \theta}{\ell \sqrt{4+\tan ^2 \theta}}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: A bike of length $\ell$ having its centre of mass at the middle is to turn with constant speed v [here v is the speed of centre of mass of the bike]. To turn, the front wheel is rotated by an angle $\theta$. The coefficient of friction between the tyres and the road is $\mu$. Assuming the bike to be vertical and it does not skid, answer the following questions.The friction force acting on the bike
questions.solution: If the bike does not slip, path of both the wheel must be parallel with its direction as shown in the figure. $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{R}_1=\ell / \tan \theta \\ & \mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{cm}}=\sqrt{\left(\frac{\ell}{2}\right)^2+\mathrm{R}_1^2}=\frac{\ell}{2 \tan \theta} \sqrt{4+\tan ^2 \theta} \\ & \text { Net friction force } \mathrm{f}=\frac{\mathrm{mv}^2}{\mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{cm}}}=\frac{2 \mathrm{~m} v^2 \tan \theta}{\ell \sqrt{4+\tan ^2 \theta}}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
